items.title: AMOS Stealer delivered via Cursor AI agent session
items.description: On April 23, 2026, Field Effect MDR identified AMOS Stealer malware delivered through a novel technique exploiting Cursor AI agent sessions running Claude Code. The attack employed social engineering to manipulate operators into prompting the AI agent to download and execute malicious AppleScript loaders. The heavily obfuscated scripts performed sandbox evasion checks, collected sensitive data inc
items.ai_summary: The AMOS Stealer malware is delivered through a novel technique exploiting Cursor AI agent sessions running Claude Code, leveraging social engineering to manipulate operators into prompting the AI agent to download and execute malicious AppleScript loaders. The attack utilizes AppleScript (T1059.002) to execute the malware, which then performs sandbox evasion checks (T1497) and collects sensitive data. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the theft of sensitive information, including authentication certificates (T1649), and can impair defenses (T1562), affecting individuals and organizations using Cursor AI agent sessions. To remediate, defenders should update their Cursor AI agent sessions to the latest version, ensure that all AppleScript loaders are verified and trusted, and implement strict access controls to prevent unauthorized execution of scripts. Additionally, monitoring for suspicious activity, such as unusual AppleScript execution or sandbox evasion attempts, can help detect and prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1497| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1649| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Cloud Atlas activity in the second half of 2025 and early 2026: new tools and a new payload
items.description: Cloud Atlas APT group targeted government organizations and commercial companies in Russia and Belarus during late 2025 and early 2026, employing phishing campaigns with malicious ZIP archives containing LNK shortcuts. The attackers deployed multiple backdoors including VBCloud for file theft and PowerShower for network reconnaissance. New tools identified include PowerCloud, which exfiltrates dat
items.ai_summary: The Cloud Atlas APT group has been observed targeting government organizations and commercial companies in Russia and Belarus through phishing campaigns, utilizing malicious ZIP archives containing LNK shortcuts to gain initial access. The attackers have deployed multiple backdoors, including VBCloud for file theft and PowerShower for network reconnaissance, with new tools such as PowerCloud being used for data exfiltration. If exploited, this vulnerability could result in significant data breaches and network compromise, with real-world impact including the theft of sensitive information and disruption of critical infrastructure. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust email filtering and employee education on phishing attacks, as well as ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches and configure firewalls to block suspicious traffic. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious network activity and implement a robust incident response plan to quickly respond to potential security incidents.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1560.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Tracking Iranian APT Screening Serpens’ 2026 Espionage Campaigns
items.description: Unit 42 researchers identified six new remote access Trojan variants deployed by Iran-nexus APT group Screening Serpens between February and April 2026, coinciding with a regional conflict starting February 28, 2026. The group targeted entities in the U.S., Israel, UAE, and other Middle Eastern locations, primarily focusing on technology sector professionals through highly tailored social engineer
items.ai_summary: The Screening Serpens APT group has been identified as the actor behind a series of espionage campaigns in 2026, utilizing six new remote access Trojan variants to target entities in the U.S., Israel, UAE, and other Middle Eastern locations. The group primarily focused on technology sector professionals, employing highly tailored social engineering tactics to gain initial access. The attack vector involved the use of T1219 (Remote Access Software), allowing the threat actors to establish a foothold within the targeted networks. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust email filtering and employee education programs to prevent initial access, as well as deploy endpoint detection and response tools to identify and mitigate potential infections. Additionally, defenders should ensure that all remote access software is properly configured and monitored, and that all employees are aware of the risks associated with social engineering tactics.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Fast and Furious - Nimbus Manticore Operations During the Iranian Conflict
items.description: The Iranian IRGC-affiliated threat actor Nimbus Manticore launched sophisticated cyber operations during Operation Epic Fury, the US military campaign against Iran beginning February 28, 2026. The campaigns targeted organizations in aviation and software sectors across the United States, Europe, and Middle East using career-themed phishing lures. For the first time, the actor employed SEO poisonin
items.ai_summary: The Nimbus Manticore threat actor, affiliated with the Iranian IRGC, has launched targeted cyber operations against organizations in the aviation and software sectors across the United States, Europe, and Middle East. The campaigns utilize career-themed phishing lures, as well as SEO poisoning for the first time, to gain initial access to victim systems. If exploited, these attacks can lead to significant data breaches and disruption of critical infrastructure, with potential real-world impact including compromised flight safety and stolen intellectual property. To remediate these threats, defenders should implement robust email filtering and user education programs, and consider implementing a web application firewall to detect and prevent SEO poisoning attacks. Additionally, organizations should ensure all employee-facing systems are updated with the latest security patches and configure their systems to use DNSSEC and HTTPS to prevent DNS spoofing and man-in-the-middle attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1608.006| C[Discovery]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1055| E[Collection]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: From edge appliance to enterprise compromise: Multi-stage Linux intrusion via F5 and Confluence
items.description: A sophisticated multi-stage intrusion began with the compromise of an internet-facing F5 BIG-IP load balancer running an end-of-life version. The threat actor established SSH access to a Linux server using privileged credentials, then conducted extensive reconnaissance including network scanning with Nmap and service enumeration with gowitness. Following horizontal and vertical scanning operations
items.ai_summary: The threat actor exploited an end-of-life F5 BIG-IP load balancer to gain initial access, then used privileged credentials to establish SSH access to a Linux server. The actor conducted extensive reconnaissance, including network scanning with Nmap and service enumeration with gowitness, to gather information about the target network. This multi-stage intrusion allowed the actor to move laterally and vertically, potentially compromising sensitive data and systems. To remediate, defenders should immediately upgrade any end-of-life F5 BIG-IP load balancers to a supported version, such as BIG-IP 17.0.0 or later, and rotate all privileged credentials. Additionally, defenders should implement robust network segmentation and monitoring to detect and prevent similar intrusions.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1595| B[Reconnaissance]:::tactic
    B -->|T1046| C[Resource Development]:::tactic
    C -->|T1213| D[Collection]:::tactic
    D -->|T1104| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Laravel Lang Compromised with RCE Backdoor Across 700+ Versions
items.description: Community-maintained Laravel Lang packages were compromised with remote code execution backdoors affecting over 700 versions across multiple repositories including laravel-lang/lang, laravel-lang/http-statuses, laravel-lang/attributes, and laravel-lang/actions. The attack involved coordinated rapid tag publishing on May 22-23, 2026, suggesting organization-level credential compromise. A malicious
items.ai_summary: The Laravel Lang package compromise involves a remote code execution (RCE) backdoor affecting over 700 versions across multiple repositories. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications (T1190) by utilizing malicious packages, which can lead to command and scripting interpreter exploitation (T1059). Real-world impact includes potential data breaches and unauthorized access to systems, with specific examples including compromised web applications and unauthorized data exfiltration. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove and replace affected Laravel Lang packages with verified, secure versions, and monitor for suspicious activity. Additionally, defenders should review and update their credential management practices to prevent similar organization-level credential compromises.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Android Trojan Abuses Commercial Rooting Tool and Steals Private Information
items.description: Rootnik is an Android trojan that exploits vulnerabilities in Android 4.3 and earlier by weaponizing a Chinese commercial rooting tool called Root Assistant. The malicious operation spreads through repackaged legitimate applications distributed globally, affecting users primarily in the United States, Malaysia, Thailand, Lebanon and Taiwan. After installation, Rootnik gains root access using stole
items.ai_summary: The Rootnik Android trojan exploits vulnerabilities in Android 4.3 and earlier versions by utilizing a commercial rooting tool called Root Assistant, allowing it to gain root access and steal private information. The trojan is distributed through repackaged legitimate applications, primarily affecting users in the United States, Malaysia, Thailand, Lebanon, and Taiwan. To exploit the vulnerability, the threat actor uses the T1068 tactic for privilege escalation, which enables the trojan to access sensitive data. Defenders can remediate this threat by ensuring their Android devices are updated to a version later than 4.3, and by verifying the authenticity of applications before installation. Additionally, users can prevent exploitation by disabling the 'Unknown Sources' setting, which restricts the installation of applications from untrusted sources.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1068| B[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    B -->|T1212| C[Exploitation of Remote Services]:::tactic
    C -->|T1082| D[System Information Discovery]:::tactic
    D -->|T1539| E[Steal Private Information]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Exploitation of KnowledgeDeliver via ViewState Deserialization Vulnerability
items.description: In late 2025, an unknown threat actor exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in KnowledgeDeliver, a Learning Management System widely used in Japan. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5426, allowed unauthenticated remote code execution through ViewState deserialization attacks. The issue stemmed from identical hardcoded ASP.NET machine keys distributed across multiple customer deployments
items.ai_summary: The exploitation of KnowledgeDeliver via ViewState deserialization vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5426, is a critical zero-day vulnerability that allows unauthenticated remote code execution. This vulnerability affects multiple customer deployments of KnowledgeDeliver, a Learning Management System widely used in Japan, due to identical hardcoded ASP.NET machine keys. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to a full system compromise, allowing threat actors to execute arbitrary code, potentially resulting in data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately update their KnowledgeDeliver deployments to the latest version, which includes a unique ASP.NET machine key for each customer, and apply the recommended configuration changes, such as enabling ViewState validation and limiting the size of ViewState data. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement a web application firewall to detect and prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic
    C -->|T1210| D[Exploitation of Remote Services]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Token Bingo: Don't Let Your Code be the Winner
items.description: In early April 2026, a large-scale device code phishing campaign targeted organizations across multiple sectors and regions, exploiting OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant. Threat actors leveraged the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform, originating primarily from IP address 216.203.20[.]95. The campaign used high-fidelity lures directing victims to Microsoft's legitimate device login flow, wh
items.ai_summary: The Token Bingo campaign exploited the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant, leveraging the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform to target organizations across multiple sectors and regions. The attack vector involved high-fidelity lures directing victims to Microsoft's legitimate device login flow, which if successful, would grant the threat actors access to sensitive information and potentially lead to further malicious activities. The real-world impact of this campaign is significant, with multiple organizations affected, and the potential for data breaches and unauthorized access to sensitive systems. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement additional security measures such as multi-factor authentication and monitor for suspicious activity originating from the IP address 216.203.20[.]95. Furthermore, users should be educated on how to identify and report phishing attempts, and organizations should consider implementing a phishing incident response plan.

    B -->|T1204| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Persistence]:::tactic

items.title: RemotePE: The Lazarus RAT that lives in memory
items.description: A sophisticated memory-only toolset used by a North Korean Lazarus subgroup targeting financial and cryptocurrency organizations consists of three malware families forming a chain. DPAPILoader decrypts and loads RemotePELoader from disk using Windows Data Protection API. RemotePELoader beacons to command-and-control servers and retrieves RemotePE, a fully-fledged remote access trojan executed enti
items.ai_summary: The RemotePE threat involves a sophisticated memory-only toolset used by the North Korean Lazarus subgroup, targeting financial and cryptocurrency organizations. This toolset consists of three malware families forming a chain, starting with DPAPILoader, which decrypts and loads RemotePELoader from disk using the Windows Data Protection API. RemotePELoader then beacons to command-and-control servers and retrieves RemotePE, a fully-fledged remote access trojan executed entirely in memory. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement network traffic monitoring to detect unusual application layer protocol activity, as indicated by T1071, and restrict remote access software, as indicated by T1219, to only trusted sources. Additionally, defenders should prioritize patching and securing any vulnerabilities that could be exploited to gain initial access, such as those related to Windows Data Protection API.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1071| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2018-25323: Allok AVI DivX MPEG to DVD Converter 2.6.1217 contains a structured exception ha...
items.description: Allok AVI DivX MPEG to DVD Converter 2.6.1217 contains a structured exception handler buffer overflow vulnerability that allows local attackers to execute arbitrary code by supplying a malicious payload. Attackers can craft a text file with a specially crafted buffer containing shellcode and SEH chain overwrite values, then paste the contents into the License Name field to trigger code execution.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25323 is a structured exception handler buffer overflow vulnerability in Allok AVI DivX MPEG to DVD Converter version 2.6.1217, allowing local attackers to execute arbitrary code by supplying a malicious payload. The attack vector involves crafting a text file with a specially crafted buffer containing shellcode and SEH chain overwrite values, then pasting the contents into the License Name field to trigger code execution. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the execution of malicious code, potentially resulting in data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, users should upgrade to a patched version of the software, such as version 2.6.1218 or later, and avoid pasting untrusted input into the License Name field. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk by implementing strict input validation and sanitization, as well as monitoring system logs for suspicious activity.
items.workflow_graph:

    B -->|T1059| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic

    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2018-25322: Allok Fast AVI MPEG Splitter 1.2 contains a stack based buffer overflow vulnerab...
items.description: Allok Fast AVI MPEG Splitter 1.2 contains a stack based buffer overflow vulnerability that allows local attackers to execute arbitrary code by supplying a malicious license name string. Attackers can craft a payload with 780 bytes of junk data followed by structured shellcode and place it in the License Name field to trigger the overflow and execute code with application privileges.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25322 is a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in Allok Fast AVI MPEG Splitter version 1.2, allowing local attackers to execute arbitrary code by supplying a malicious license name string. The attack vector involves crafting a payload with 780 bytes of junk data followed by structured shellcode and placing it in the License Name field to trigger the overflow. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to code execution with application privileges, potentially affecting users who have installed the vulnerable version of the software. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to a version later than 1.2, and as a specific step, ensure that the License Name field input is validated and sanitized to prevent malicious input. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk by running the application with reduced privileges, thereby limiting the potential impact of a successful exploit.
items.workflow_graph:

items.title: CVE-2018-25321: TP-Link TL-WR720N wireless router contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerab...
items.description: TP-Link TL-WR720N wireless router contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allows attackers to perform unauthorized administrative actions by crafting malicious web requests. Attackers can modify port forwarding rules via VirtualServerRpm.htm or change WiFi security settings via WlanSecurityRpm.htm by tricking authenticated users into visiting attacker-controlled pages.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25321 is a cross-site request forgery vulnerability in TP-Link TL-WR720N wireless routers, allowing attackers to perform unauthorized administrative actions by tricking authenticated users into visiting malicious web pages. The vulnerability can be exploited by crafting malicious web requests to modify port forwarding rules via VirtualServerRpm.htm or change WiFi security settings via WlanSecurityRpm.htm. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to the network, allowing attackers to intercept sensitive data or launch further attacks. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update their TP-Link TL-WR720N wireless router to the latest firmware version and ensure that the 'Remote Management' feature is disabled. Additionally, users should be cautious when clicking on links from unknown sources and ensure that their browser and operating system are up-to-date with the latest security patches.
items.workflow_graph:

    B -->|T1204| C[User Execution]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2018-25320: ACL Analytics versions 11.x through 13.0.0.579 contain an arbitrary code executi...
items.description: ACL Analytics versions 11.x through 13.0.0.579 contain an arbitrary code execution vulnerability that allows attackers to execute arbitrary commands by leveraging the EXECUTE function. Attackers can use bitsadmin to download malicious PowerShell scripts and execute them with system privileges to establish reverse shells and gain complete system control.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25320 is an arbitrary code execution vulnerability in ACL Analytics versions 11.x through 13.0.0.579, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary commands by leveraging the EXECUTE function. This vulnerability can be exploited by attackers to download malicious PowerShell scripts using bitsadmin and execute them with system privileges, establishing reverse shells and gaining complete system control. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is high, as it can lead to complete system compromise, with potential targets including financial institutions and government agencies. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update ACL Analytics to version 13.0.0.580 or later, and restrict access to the EXECUTE function to authorized personnel only. Additionally, defenders should monitor system logs for suspicious bitsadmin activity and PowerShell script execution, and implement a web application firewall to detect and prevent malicious script uploads.
items.workflow_graph:

items.title: CVE-2018-25319: Redaxo CMS Addon MyEvents 2.2.1 contains an SQL injection vulnerability that all...
items.description: Redaxo CMS Addon MyEvents 2.2.1 contains an SQL injection vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to manipulate database queries by injecting SQL code through the myevents_id parameter. Attackers can send GET requests to the event_add.php page with malicious myevents_id values to extract or modify sensitive database information.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25319 is an SQL injection vulnerability in Redaxo CMS Addon MyEvents 2.2.1, allowing authenticated attackers to inject malicious SQL code through the myevents_id parameter in event_add.php. This can lead to sensitive database information extraction or modification. To remediate, update to Redaxo CMS Addon MyEvents 2.2.2 or later, and ensure the myevents_id parameter is properly sanitized. This vulnerability was exploited using TTP T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application). Defenders should monitor for GET requests to event_add.php with suspicious myevents_id values. Additionally, consider implementing web application firewalls (WAFs) to block malicious traffic.

items.description: A security flaw has been discovered in h2oai h2o-3 up to 7402. This affects the function importBinaryModel of the file h2o-core/src/main/java/hex/Model.java of the component JAR Handler. Performing a manipulation results in deserialization. items.title: CVE-2026-8750: A vulnerability was identified in h2oai h2o-3 up to 7402. Affected by this issue...
items.description: A vulnerability was identified in h2oai h2o-3 up to 7402. Affected by this issue is the function importFiles of the file h2o-core/src/main/java/water/persist/PersistNFS.java of the component ImportFile API. Such manipulation leads to information disclosure. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure

items.title: CVE-2026-8747: A weakness has been identified in Z-BlogPHP 1.7.4.3430. This affects the functio...
items.description: A weakness has been identified in Z-BlogPHP 1.7.4.3430. This affects the function CheckComment of the file zb_system/function/c_system_event.php of the component Commend Approval Handler. This manipulation causes improper authorization. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-8746: A security flaw has been discovered in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected by this iss...
items.description: A security flaw has been discovered in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected by this issue is the function discover_handler in the library /lib/sbi/nghttp2-server.c of the component NRF. The manipulation results in use after free. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue repo

items.title: The Credential Crisis: How Stolen Credentials Defeat Modern Security
items.description: As AI accelerates phishing, session hijacking, and credential abuse, security teams are racing to close the gap between attacker speed and defensive response. The post The Credential Crisis: How Stolen Credentials Defeat Modern Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ‘SymJack’ Attack Turns AI Coding Agents Into Supply Chain Attack Delivery Systems
items.description: Malicious repositories and disguised symlinks can trick AI coding agents into silently installing attacker-controlled MCP servers capable of stealing secrets, compromising CI pipelines, and deploying malicious code. The post ‘SymJack’ Attack Turns AI Coding Agents Into Supply Chain Attack Delivery Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: GlassWorm Botnet Disrupted
items.description: Security firms took down all four command-and-control (C&C) channels used by the GlassWorm malware. The post GlassWorm Botnet Disrupted appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA gives feds 4 days to patch actively exploited cPanel plugin flaw
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has given U.S. federal agencies four days to secure their servers against a critical vulnerability in the LiteSpeed cPanel user-end plugin, which is actively being exploited in attacks. [...]

items.title: LA Metro Cyberattack Linked to Iranian State-Sponsored Hackers
items.description: The attack was claimed by a hacktivist group, but evidence showed it used infrastructure linked to Iranian government threat actors. The post LA Metro Cyberattack Linked to Iranian State-Sponsored Hackers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Dutch police arrests suspect linked to Ajax football club hack
items.description: The Dutch National Police arrested a 35-year-old man suspected of hacking the professional football club Ajax Amsterdam (AFC Ajax) earlier this year. [...]

items.title: Windows 11 KB5089573 update released with performance improvements
items.description: Microsoft has released the KB5089573 preview cumulative update for Windows 11 versions 25H2 and 24H2, which comes with 30 changes, including performance and reliability improvements. [...]

items.title: FBI: Hackers Sending Operatives in Person to Insert USB Drives and Steal Data
items.description: The FBI has issued an alert warning of Silent Ransom Group attacks targeting law firms. The post FBI: Hackers Sending Operatives in Person to Insert USB Drives and Steal Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI Chatbot Recommendations Redirect Users to Cryptojacking Malware Sites
items.description: Microsoft has warned of an active cryptojacking campaign that makes use of artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot interactions as a mechanism for surfacing malicious download sites. "This emerging delivery technique extends social engineering beyond conventional search results and increases the visibility of malicious software recommendations," Microsoft Defender Experts and the Microsoft

items.title: CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Zero-Day
items.description: Resolved last week, the vulnerability was exploited in the wild as a zero-day to execute scripts with root privileges. The post CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anthropic Releases New Claude Sandbox, Security Guidance Plugin
items.description: The AI giant says the new plugin, which helps developers find vulnerabilities as they write code, has been used extensively internally. The post Anthropic Releases New Claude Sandbox, Security Guidance Plugin appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 27th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9946, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: KnowledgeDeliver flaw exploited as a zero-day to install web shells
items.description: Hackers exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in a server running the KnowledgeDeliver learning management system (LMS) to deploy the Godzilla web shell. [...]

items.title: Feeding Frenzy: 'Megalodon' Malware Infects Thousands of GitHub Repos
items.description: In just six hours, the campaign quietly pushed thousands of malicious commits to more than 5,500 GitHub repositories, stealing credentials, developer secrets, and more.

items.title: Charter confirms data breach after ShinyHunters extortion threat
items.description: U.S. telecommunications giant Charter Communications has confirmed it suffered a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group threatened to leak stolen data unless a ransom is paid. [...]

items.title: The Hackers Behind Shai-Hulud: Lucky or Skilled?
items.description: TeamPCP, the hackers behind the Shai-Hulud worm, has done significant damage to the open source ecosystem. But it's not necessarily due to skill alone.

items.title: Microsoft Issues Out-of-Band SharePoint Patch
items.description: SharePoint access often means access to the keys of the kingdom, something attackers and defenders understand all too well.

items.title: MuddyWater Uses DLL Side-Loading in Espionage Campaign Targeting 9 Countries
items.description: The Iranian hacking group known as MuddyWater has been linked to a new campaign affecting at least nine organizations across nine countries on four continents in the first quarter of 2026. The activity targeted industrial and electronics manufacturing, education and public-sector bodies, financial services, and professional services, per the Threat Hunter Team from Symantec and Carbon Black.

items.title: Lithuania investigates theft of 600,000 state registry records by foreign actor
items.description: The Lithuanian Prosecutor General’s Office said Friday that attackers gained unauthorized access to more than 600,000 records managed by the Centre of Registers, the state agency responsible for handling property and legal entity records.

items.title: How Varonis Atlas integrates Claude Compliance API for AI governance
items.description: AI governance requires visibility into how AI tools interact with enterprise data. Varonis explains how its Atlas platform uses Claude Compliance API data to help monitor usage, investigate risk, and support compliance. [...]

items.title: AppOmni’s Marlin AI Brings Autonomous Investigation to SaaS Security
items.description: Marlin AI automatically analyzes SaaS misconfigurations, investigates related activity across enterprise environments, and recommends remediation steps — while stopping short of fully autonomous corrective action. The post AppOmni’s Marlin AI Brings Autonomous Investigation to SaaS Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Iranian APT Targets Aviation, Software Companies With Updated Tools
items.description: Nimbus Manticore has continued its operations during and after the US military campaign against Iran. The post Iranian APT Targets Aviation, Software Companies With Updated Tools appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Defender can now automatically isolate hacked endpoints
items.description: Microsoft is testing a new Defender for Endpoint capability that will automatically isolate compromised endpoints to thwart attackers' attempts to move laterally across the network. [...]

items.title: Webinar: Too many tools are slowing network incident response
items.description: IT teams often need to jump between monitoring dashboards, infrastructure tools, ticketing systems, and communication platforms during network incidents. This webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workflows can help reduce manual coordination and improve incident response times. [...]

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Remembering Tim Wilson, Whose Legacy Lives on at Dark Reading
items.description: The co-founder and former editor-in-chief passed away five years ago in November. As Dark Reading enters its third decade, we pause to celebrate and honor Wilson's instrumental role in building and elevating the media site.

items.title: 185,000 Likely Impacted by 7-Eleven Data Breach
items.description: The allegedly stolen information leaked by ShinyHunters contains email addresses, names, addresses, and dates of birth. The post 185,000 Likely Impacted by 7-Eleven Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: [THN Webinar] New AI DDoS Attacks Are Smarter. Learn How to Fight Back
items.description: Every single day, hackers are finding new ways to crash websites and steal data. But right now, something has changed. Hackers are no longer working alone. They are now using powerful Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to make their attacks faster, stronger, and much harder to stop. According to recent updates from The Hacker News, bad actors are using AI to find weak spots in systems and

items.title: Microsoft Patches SharePoint RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45659 Across Server Versions
items.description: Microsoft has rolled out updates to fix a remote code execution vulnerability impacting SharePoint that could be exploited by bad actors in attacks without requiring any specialized conditions to be met. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-45659, carries a CVSS score of 8.8. It has been assigned an important severity. "Deserialization of untrusted data in Microsoft Office SharePoint allows

items.title: MFA Prompt Bombing: Why Your Second Factor Isn't Saving You
items.description: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) was supposed to close a critical gap in identity security. It meant that, even if an attacker possessed the account credentials, they couldn't log in without the second factor. While that logic was sound, attackers have now figured out that they don't need to steal the second factor: they just need the user to hand it over. If your workforce authenticates with

items.title: CERT-In Recommends 12-Hour Patching for Internet-Facing Flaws Amid AI-Assisted Attacks
items.description: The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has issued new guidelines requiring organizations to patch critical security vulnerabilities in internet-exposed systems within 12 hours of being flagged where "feasible" to safeguard against potential threats stemming from threat actors' abuse of artificial intelligence (AI) tools and large language models (LLMs) to automate vulnerability

items.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Drupal vulnerability
items.description: CISA has given U.S. government agencies until Wednesday evening to secure their servers against an SQL injection vulnerability in the Drupal content management system (CMS) that it flagged as actively exploited. [...]

items.title: Microsoft: Domain Controller lookup may fail on Windows Server 2016
items.description: Microsoft has confirmed a new known issue affecting Windows Server 2016 systems that causes domain controller lookups to fail after installing the KB5087537 May 2026 security update. [...]

items.title: Iranian Hackers Deploy MiniFast and MiniJunk V2 via Phishing and SEO Poisoning
items.description: The Iranian state-sponsored threat actor known as Nimbus Manticore (aka Screening Serpens and UNC1549) has been attributed to a fresh campaign using lures impersonating organizations in the aviation and software sectors across the U.S., Europe, and the Middle East following the joint U.S.-Israeli military campaign against the country in late February 2026. The activity, besides embracing

items.title: KnowledgeDeliver LMS Flaw Exploited to Deploy Godzilla and Cobalt Strike
items.description: A now-patched high-severity security flaw affecting Digital Knowledge KnowledgeDeliver, a Learning Management System (LMS) popular in Japan, was exploited as a zero-day to deliver the Godzilla web shell and ultimately facilitate the deployment of Cobalt Strike Beacon. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5426 (CVSS score: 7.5), stems from the use of hard-coded ASP.NET machine keys, leading to

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 26th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9944, (Tue, May 26th)

items.title: Possible ACR Stealer From Page Impersonating Claude, (Tue, May 26th)
items.description: Introduction

items.title: Dutch authorities arrest men suspected of providing infrastructure for Russian cyber operations
items.description: Investigators seized more than 800 servers as they arrested two men suspected of violating European sanctions and assisting pro-Russian cyberattacks and disinformation campaigns.

items.title: Kremlin appoints cyber executive with alleged GRU ties to Security Council role
items.description: Andrei Kozlov, the former head of a cybersecurity center within Russia’s state-owned defense conglomerate Rostec, was named an aide to Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu on Friday.

items.title: Microsoft Access VBA, (Mon, May 25th)
items.description: Microsoft Access files (Microsoft Office&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Database) can contain VBA code.

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Flaws, Defender 0-Days, Router Botnets, and Supply Chain Chaos
items.description: Monday recap. Same mess, new week. A sketchy dev tool got people pwned, old bugs came back from the dead, and security products somehow needed protecting from themselves. A bunch of companies spent the week checking old boxes and forgotten servers they should've patched years ago. Good times. Phishing crews are getting smarter too - less obvious scam junk, more targeted stuff that actually

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-24, (Mon, May 25th)
items.description: TeamPCP now operates across three package ecosystems in parallel, it reached GitHub&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s own internal codebase, it trojanized an officially Microsoft-published Python SDK, and it appears to have open-sourced its own framework on GitHub.

items.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Ghost CMS CVE-2026-26980 Exploited to Hijack 700+ Sites for ClickFix Attacks
items.description: Threat actors are exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Ghost CMS to inject malicious JavaScript code with an aim to fuel ClickFix attacks. According to QiAnXin XLab, the activity involves the exploitation of CVE-2026-26980 (CVSS score: 9.4), an SQL injection vulnerability in Ghost's Content API that could allow an unauthenticated attacker to read arbitrary data from the

items.title: Wireshark 4.6.6 Released, (Sun, May 24th)
items.description: Wireshark release 4.6.6 fixes 1 vulnerability and 11 bugs.

items.title: An Example of Stack String in High Level Language, (Sat, May 23rd)
items.description: This week, I&#x27m attending the SEC670[1] training (â€œRed Teaming Tools - Developing Windows Implants, Shellcode, Command and Controlâ€). From my point of view, this training fits perfectly with FOR610 or FOR710 (malware analysis) because it addresses malware from the opposite: Instead of performing reverse engineering, you write malicious code! Always interesting to have another point of view.

items.title: CISA to allow researchers to report vulnerabilities to exploited bugs catalog
items.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) announced the creation of a nomination form on Thursday that they said enables “researchers, vendors, and industry partners” to report bugs that need to be added to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

items.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing-as-a-service after April Microsoft 365 attacks
items.description: The law enforcement agency published an advisory on Thursday about Kali365 — a Telegram-based service for cybercriminals that allows them to capture legitimate "OAuth" tokens enabling widespread access to Microsoft 365 environments.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Akamai Joins Growing Chorus of Vendors Betting Big on Secure Enterprise Browsers
items.description: When Akamai announced its LayerX acquisition, the company joined a growing list of vendors adding secure enterprise browsers to their product portfolios.

items.title: Verizon DBIR: Healthcare Fends Off Increased Social Engineering Attacks
items.description: Ransomware and vendor breaches persist. The "2026 Data Breach Investigations Report" (DBIR) highlights how evolving social engineering tactics make the sector more vulnerable.

items.title: China's Webworm Uses Discord, Microsoft Graphs to Hack EU Governments
items.description: The advanced persistent threat group also relied on SOCKS proxies like SoftEther VPN, tunneling tools that act as a middleman between victim and attacker.

items.title: Cross-Platform NPM Stealer, (Fri, May 22nd)
items.description: I found a Node.js stealer that looked pretty well obfuscated. The file was not running out-of-the-box because it was uploaded on VT as â€œextracted-decoded.jsâ€ (and reformated). The SHA256 is 049300aa5dd774d6c984779a0570f59610399c71864b5d5c2605906db46ddeb9[1]. It did not run properly in a sandbox so only a static analysis was performed.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 22nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9942, (Fri, May 22nd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: Google API Keys Remain Active After Deletion
items.description: A security researcher discovered the API keys can still be used for up to 23 minutes after deletion, even though the cloud provider claims deletion is immediate.

items.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
items.description: AI agent projects are proliferating throughout the enterprise, and those AI agent identities require management, security, and governance. New Omdia research shows the AI agent identity budget dynamics are very different than traditional IAM projects.

items.title: Chinese APTs Share Linux Backdoor in Central Asia Telco Attacks
items.description: "Showboat" doesn't show off, but clearly it doesn't need to, as it's long helped China spy on small market communications providers.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
